memoryArtifacts.description: Sir John Carrington spent a large part  of his life in disguise as he fled to France, upon the disposal of Richard II, for taking up arms with Earls of Huntington, Salifbury and Kent against Henry IV …

nameMeanings.name: Smythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John William Smythe was born in 1356, in Rivenhall, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington, was 21 and his mother, Lady Margaret de Ros, was 16. He had at least 5 sons with Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham. He died in 1446, in his hometown, at the age of 90, and was buried in Rivenhall, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John William Smythe est né en 1356, à Rivenhall, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret de Ros, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils avec Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham. Il est décédé en 1446, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 90 ans, et a été enterré à Rivenhall, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John William Smythe 1356 in Rivenhall, Essex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington 21 und seine Mutter Lady Margaret de Ros 16. Er hatte mit Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb 1446 im Alter von 90 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Rivenhall, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John William Smythe è nato il nel 1356 nel Rivenhall, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Lady Margaret de Ros, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli con Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham. Morì nel 1446, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 90 anni, e fu sepolto a Rivenhall, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John William Smythe(이)가 1356년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 라이븐홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret de Ros(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1446년 그의 고향에서 90세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 라이븐홀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John William Smythe nasceu em 1356, em Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret de Ros, tinha 16 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos com Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham. Ele faleceu em 1446, em sua cidade natal, com 90 anos, e foi sepultado em Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John William Smythe родился в 1356 г. в местности ривенолл, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret de Ros, 16. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей от Lady Millicent Elizabeth Laingham. Он умер в 1446 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 90 лет и был похоронен в местности ривенолл, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John William Smythe nació en 1356, en Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret de Ros, tenía 16 años.
